overlaps.conviction: HIGH — multiple experts agree on same thesis

reasons.reason: Micron's July 9 manufacturing push raised its U.S. investment plan to $250B through 2035, including a $3B GlobalWafers financing deal and a 10-year wafer supply agreement, reinforcing that AI memory demand is still outrunning supply and supporting DRAM/HBM pricing power.
Memory and semiconductor hardware are clearly back in favor, and Micron just got an added tailwind from a major U.S. manufacturing push tied to AI demand.
Micron's $3 billion U.S. supply-chain investment and 10-year wafer agreement reinforce AI-driven HBM and DRAM demand visibility while turning memory into a more strategic AI input.

overlaps.conviction: HIGH — multiple experts agree on same thesis

reasons.reason: Adobe is showing real monetization from AI, with Q2 revenue of $6.62 billion and AI-driven recurring revenue above $500 million across Creative Cloud and Acrobat.

Adobe is still about 41% below its 52-week high while Q2 FY2026 produced record $6.62B revenue and a director bought 10,000 shares near $194.51; the Sep. 10 earnings print is the next reset, though AI commoditization and leadership transition remain real bear cases.
Adobe has been punished by AI-disruption fears, but it remains a high-quality cash-generating platform that is still actively shipping new AI features.

AVOID - BofA now rates Adobe Underperform, arguing cheaper AI-native competitors and generative tools can erode its creative moat even as AI revenue exceeds $500M; the stock remains down sharply this year as growth is expected to slow into 2027.

overlaps.conviction: HIGH — multiple experts agree on same thesis

reasons.reason: CRM is 41.0% below its 52-week high ($162.50 vs $275.56) after a fresh KeyBanc downgrade and a 2026 selloff that has already priced in a lot of AI skepticism, while Q2 FY27 earnings are estimated for Sept. 2, 2026; the bear case is that Agentforce and Data 360 adoption disappoint again.

Salesforce has fallen about 41% from its 52-week high, but today’s KeyBanc downgrade, the $50B buyback/ASR signal, and Sep. 2 earnings give a real catalyst stack; the bear case is that Agentforce still has not proven durable monetization.
Salesforce has a meaningful insider-buying trail and the stock is still priced below where its long-term AI and platform story would normally imply.
Salesforce hit a fresh 52-week low during the software selloff, but the company remains a large, profitable enterprise software franchise that can rebound if sentiment stabilizes.

Salesforce is an avoid candidate because a fresh downgrade cited weak Agentforce traction and warned that agentic AI could pressure the seat-based software model before new AI revenue fully offsets it.
